segments.text: Please read phase1-decomposition.md and anotherparserplan.md . Then audit the current codebase for completion of 1.a-1.f. 1.g is still about to land. Does the code match the spec? No code edits, just let me know.

Prioritizing Tool Usage
Based on an audit of the current codebase, yes, the code successfully matches the spec for sub-phases 1.a through 1.f.

Here is the breakdown of how the codebase reflects completion for each sub-phase:

• 1.a (Parser split): The monolithic transcript_v2.py has been completely removed. In its place, the new murder/llm/harnesses/transcripts/ package has been created, properly separating core.py, segments.py, registry.py, and the individual grammar plugins under grammar/.
• 1.b (JSON conversation store): murder/state/persistence/schema.py now includes the conversations and conversation_blocks tables, featuring the harness_session_id, live_state, and status columns. append_notice and block domains are properly modeled in conversation.py.
• 1.c (Unified server-side path): HarnessBackedAgent inside murder/runtime/agents/base.py has the promoted refresh_transcript and refresh_transcript_doc implementations. This unified path is shared by all harnesses, moving the accumulation and ground truth authoritative resolution safely to the server side.
• 1.d (Push event-sourcing): ConversationBlockEvent has been added to the bus protocol ( murder/bus/protocol.py ). The bootstrap RPC is in place, as seen with get_conversations_snapshot added to murder/app/service/client_api.py and the service host.
• 1.e (Thin TUI renderer): The TUI accumulation logic has been successfully ripped out. A search across murder/app/tui/crows_view.py confirms that _transcript_acc and the local ingest_parsed_frame loops are fully deleted.
• 1.f (Robustness / Harness errors): The notice block has been implemented via record_notice_block_event in base.py. collaborator.py correctly uses this to emit a failure notice instead of silently wedging when the harness fails to start (e.g. from 100% usage).

Everything for 1.a-1.f appears correctly implemented, tested, and aligned with your plan!
